food_subgroup: Flavoured tea drinks with probiotic dairy

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for lychee-flavoured Yakult tea blend. FOODON_03301099 (tea beverage) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000 (Tea and tea-based beverages) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for lychee Yakult tea blend.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 74242007 (Tea substance) used as closest parent; no specific code for lychee Yakult tea blend.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 ml (400 g), equivalent to a regular-sized takeaway cup as typically served at bubble tea and specialty tea shops in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: Yakult Original (USDA FoodData Central NDB 45358724), brewed green/black tea (USDA SR Legacy 14355/14214), lychee juice/syrup (USDA SR Legacy 09164), and sugar syrup; proportions based on standard Singapore tea-shop recipe (approx. 65 ml Yakult, 200 ml tea, 80 ml lychee juice/syrup, 55 ml ice water, 10 g sugar syrup per 400 ml serving).
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Lychee Yakult Tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA. Values estimated by proportional blending of approved USDA source entries for each component ingredient. Yakult Original per 65 ml: ~50 kcal, 1.0 g protein, 0 g fat, 11.5 g carbs, 10.5 g sugar, 20 mg sodium. Brewed tea per 200 ml: ~2 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0.5 g carbs, 0 g sugar, 7 mg sodium. Lychee juice/syrup per 80 ml: ~50 kcal, 0.4 g protein, 0.1 g fat, 13 g carbs, 12 g sugar, 1 mg sodium. Sugar syrup per 10 g: ~30 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 8 g carbs, 8 g sugar, 0 mg sodium. Total per 400 g serving: ~132 kcal, 1.4 g protein, 0.1 g fat, 33 g carbs, 30.5 g sugar, 28 mg sodium. Per 100 g: ~33 kcal, 0.35 g protein, 0.025 g fat, 8.25 g carbs, 7.6 g sugar, 7 mg sodium.

gi_source.title: Fructose-containing beverages and lychee juice GI analogy; Yakult probiotic drink GI reference; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Lychee Yakult Tea. GI estimated at 52 based on weighted analogy: Yakult Original GI ~46 (University of Sydney GI Database, probiotic milk drink), lychee juice GI ~50 (estimated from fructose-rich tropical fruit juice analogues in Atkinson et al. 2008), brewed tea GI ~1 (effectively zero, no glycemic carbohydrates), sugar syrup GI ~65. Weighted blend across serving proportions yields estimated GI ~52, classified as low (≤55). GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 33.0) / 100 = 17.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~28 mg per 400 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Lychee Yakult Tea is low in sodium (~7 mg per 100 g; ~28 mg per serving), making it generally acceptable for individuals managing hypertension, though added sugar content should be monitored as excess sugar intake can indirectly elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~30.5 g free sugars per 400 ml serving) and moderate glycemic load (GL ~17)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 33 g of carbohydrates and a glycemic load of ~17 per serving, Lychee Yakult Tea poses a moderate glycemic burden for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control and reduced-sugar customisation (e.g., 0% sugar option) are strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and low sodium; moderate free sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lychee Yakult Tea contains negligible saturated fat (<0.05 g per 100 g) and low sodium, which are favourable for cardiovascular health; however, the free sugar content (~30 g per serving) warrants moderation as chronic high sugar intake is associated with dyslipidaemia and increased cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee Yakult Green/Black Tea (荔枝养乐多绿/红茶) is a popular Singaporean specialty tea-shop beverage that combines the sweet, floral flavour of lychee with the tangy probiotic character of Yakult (a Japanese fermented skimmed-milk drink containing Lactobacillus casei Shirota) and the light astringency of brewed green or black tea. The drink is typically prepared by mixing approximately 65 ml of Yakult Original with brewed green or black tea, lychee juice or syrup, ice, and optional sugar syrup, then shaken or stirred and served cold in a sealed takeaway cup. The flavour profile is refreshingly sweet-tart with a subtle milky creaminess from the Yakult and a delicate floral lychee aroma. The texture is light and liquid, similar to a lightly sweetened iced tea with a faint probiotic tang. Nutritionally, a standard 400 ml serving provides approximately 132 kcal, 33 g of carbohydrates (predominantly free sugars ~30.5 g), 1.4 g of protein from the Yakult dairy component, and negligible fat. The sodium content is low (~28 mg per serving). The glycemic index is estimated at ~52 (low GI) with a glycemic load of ~17 per serving, indicating a moderate glycemic impact primarily driven by the lychee syrup and Yakult sugar content. Clinically, this beverage is low in fat and sodium but high in free sugars, making it a moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those monitoring caloric intake. The Yakult component contributes live probiotic cultures (Lactobacillus casei Shirota), which may support gut microbiome health. This drink is widely available at Chinese-style bubble tea and specialty tea shops across Singapore, including chains such as Happy Cup, and is a signature item in the lychee-probiotic tea category.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry for Lychee Yakult Tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Values are estimated by proportional blending of approved USDA SR Legacy and Branded Food entries for each component ingredient (Yakult Original, brewed green/black tea, lychee juice, sugar syrup) using a standard Singapore tea-shop recipe proportion. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for Yakult and fructose-rich fruit juice analogues. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central approved sources; no direct HPB or USDA entry exists for this specific beverage. 2. The food name 'G/B' in the source record refers to the customer's choice of Green or Black tea base; the canonical name reflects both options. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to 0.0 as the fat content is negligible (<0.03 g per 100 g) and individual fatty acid fractions are below detection threshold — confirmed zero by component analysis. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as none of the component ingredients contain trans fats — confirmed zero by formulation. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source entries for these beverage components. 5. GI set to 52 (low, ≤55) as an analogous estimate; glycemic_load_per_serving = 17.2 calculated as (52 × 33.0 g carbs per 400 g serving) / 100. 6. Allergen: contains milk (Yakult is a fermented skimmed-milk product). 7. Price from field survey data source: SGD 11.40 (single restaurant, Happy Cup - Funan). 8. This record is cuisine-tagged as Chinese/Singaporean; multilingual names are provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements.
